thinking.text:
The script will run OCR on all 951 HQ frames, but that will take a very long time. Let me think about a better approach.

Actually, I have the 190 1fps frame OCR outputs already. The problem is that I need to properly track the sequence of commands as they appear in the transcript. Let me look at the data more carefully.

Looking at the OCR outputs, the transcript shows commands in order from oldest (top) to newest (bottom). New commands appear at the bottom of the transcript. When the player types a command, it appears as the last '>' line. After execution, it moves up into the transcript area and a new typing prompt appears.

The approach should be:
1. For each frame, get all '>' lines
2. The last '>' line is the command being typed (current input)
3. The previous '>' lines are the transcript history
4. Track when the current typing changes - that means the previous command was just executed

Let me implement this properly by tracking the typing command across frames and only recording a command when it's fully entered (the typing changes to something else, or the command shifts from typing to history).

Actually, I think the best approach is:
1. Look at all frames and track the LAST '>' line (typing)
2. When the typing changes from frame to frame, the previous typing was a completed command
3. This should give us the exact sequence of commands

Let me implement this and run it on the 190 OCR outputs.
